---
title: "imputation_genotyping_qcs"
output: html_document
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---

#"`r snakemake@params[['batch']]`

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r kinship, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=5, out.width="100%"}
library(reshape2)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

kin_data <- read.table(snakemake@input[["kinship"]],
                           header = TRUE,
                           comment.char = "")
colnames(kin_data)[1] <- "IID1"

snp_num = kin_data[1,"NSNP"]

kin_mirrored <- data.frame(IID1 = c(kin_data$IID1, kin_data$IID2),
                           IID2 = c(kin_data$IID2, kin_data$IID1),
                           KINSHIP = c(kin_data$KINSHIP, kin_data$KINSHIP)
                           )

u_ids <- unique(c(kin_data$IID1, kin_data$IID2))
self_df <- data.frame(IID1 = u_ids, IID2 = u_ids, KINSHIP = 0)

kin_full <- rbind(kin_mirrored, self_df)

kin_full$KINSHIP <- round(kin_full$KINSHIP,2)

ggplot(kin_full, aes(x = IID1, y = IID2, fill = KINSHIP)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = KINSHIP), size = 4) + # 
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "firebrick") +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = paste("Pairwise Kinship Matrix (", snp_num, " SNPs)"), fill = "Kinship")

```


```{r pca, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=5, out.width="100%"}
library(patchwork)
library(ggrepel)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)

eigenval <- read.table(snakemake@input[["eigenval"]],
		       header = FALSE)
eigenvec <- read.table(snakemake@input[["eigenvec"]],
		       header = TRUE,
		       comment.char="")

colnames(eigenvec)[1] <- "IID"


pc1_var <- round(eigenval$V1[1] / sum(eigenval$V1) * 100, 1)
pc2_var <- round(eigenval$V1[2] / sum(eigenval$V1) * 100, 1)

eigenvec$Category <- "Standard Sample"
eigenvec$Category[eigenvec$IID == "unclassified"] <- "Unclassified"
eigenvec$Category[eigenvec$IID == "01-09"] <- "Dropout"

p3 <- ggplot(eigenvec, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Category)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = IID), size = 3.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey70") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey70") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Standard Sample" = "#2c3e50",
    "Unclassified" = "#e74c3c",
    "Dropout" = "#f39c12"
  )) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  # Lock the window range from -1 to 1 for both axes
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "PCA: Genetic Convergence",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", pc1_var, "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", pc2_var, "%)"),
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

p4 <- ggplot(eigenvec, aes(x = PC9, y = PC10, color = Category)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = IID), size = 3.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey70") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey70") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Standard Sample" = "#2c3e50",
    "Unclassified" = "#e74c3c",
    "Dropout" = "#f39c12"
  )) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  # Lock the window range from -1 to 1 for both axes
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "PCA: Outlier Test",
    x = "PC9",
    y = "PC10",
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

p3 + p4


# Option A: A realistic Sigma threshold for small datasets (e.g., 2.5 or 3)
threshold <- 2.5

outliers_sigma <- eigenvec %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    abs(PC1 - mean(PC1)) / sd(PC1) > threshold |
    abs(PC2 - mean(PC2)) / sd(PC2) > threshold
  )

# Option B: The Boxplot Rule (1.5 * IQR) - Highly recommended for small n
get_iqr_outliers <- function(x) {
  q <- quantile(x, probs=c(.25, .75), na.rm = TRUE)
  iqr <- IQR(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  return(x < (q[1] - 1.5*iqr) | x > (q[2] + 1.5*iqr))
}

outliers_iqr <- eigenvec %>%
  dplyr::filter(get_iqr_outliers(PC1) | get_iqr_outliers(PC2))

print("Outliers detected via IQR:")
print(outliers_iqr$IID)
```

```{r ratio, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=5, out.width="100%"}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

df_nhet_nhomalt <- read.table(snakemake@input[["ratios"]], sep = "\t", header = TRUE)

df_nhet_nhomalt$nHetsnHomsAltRatio <- round( df_nhet_nhomalt$Heterozygous.0.1. / df_nhet_nhomalt$nHomAlt.1.1.,2)

colnames(df_nhet_nhomalt) <- c("Sample","nRefHom","nHomAlt","nHets","Ratio")

ggplot(df_nhet_nhomalt, aes(x = Sample, y = Ratio, fill = Sample == "unclassified")) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "#e74c3c", "FALSE" = "#7f8c8d")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), legend.position = "none") +
  labs(title = "Het/Hom-Alt Ratio", y = "Ratio", x = "")
```
